title: stipa columbiana subsp. nelsonii
intro: Stipa columbiana subsp. nelsonii, also known as Nelson's needle grass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is native to the western United States and is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and bronchodilator effects. Herbal preparations of Nelson's needle grass can be made by infusing the dried leaves and stems in hot water, and it is often used in teas and infusions to promote respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
